reasoning: Candidate (right, 4x on parchment) presents warm tan/sandy brick stonework with scattered pink highlights and golden accents. Avoids all hard-fail categories cleanly: no cold grey dungeon stone, no blood/skulls, no sci-fi/corporate palette, no desaturation, no AAA muddy realism. The warm tan base and rosy-pink accents are fairy-tale friendly and not ungracious for a Hyrule-style outdoor wall. However, overall saturation is somewhat muted and there is a faint dusty neutrality that flirts with 'pleasant beige' rather than 'bright sunny Hyrule'. No sunny greens appear anywhere — acceptable for a wall tile but noted absence. Pink speckling reads as flowering vine growth which is charming and on-tone. Peer reference (left) is starkly grimdark dungeon with red blood pools, cold grey stones, and occult-adjacent teal runes — candidate is unambiguously warmer and friendlier by comparison. Scores marginal rather than pass because it needs slightly more saturation pop and warmth to fully commit to the Hyrule outdoor register.
